body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: An update on the updating the registries draft

you don't have any sense of time at all you should be able to boot snap a clock so you can do CER verifi validate T certificate so that you can run dnsc and I I sort of believe that we need some kind of way to boost trp the protocol it might be possible to get this working with NTS in a secure way boot rough time is a different way of doing it where you basically hardcode a list of public keys that you trust so it's a different way of doing things and I I kind of like the time idea I also work a lot with come from the embedded systems part so I like the idea of being able to have a small like device like this and get it a small device that might not have the resources to run TLS which is need by NTS I'd like the idea of having a lightweight time protocol secure time protocol for device like these so I I see two really sort of useful places for something like rough time unfortunately rough time right now now is kind of stalled a lot of the people who have been working on rough time they have other things to do and they haven't had time for this for example Marcus danar is doing his PhD and he he's he wants to finish that one and but I work for net node net node as a company are very interested in rough time we want to do something with this I'm personally from the better side I'm very interested in rough time so we will actually from net no try to spend money actual money on getting people together in the few in the sort of next few months and actually start working on rough time so if you are interested in rough time or a protocol like it it it might be better to say well maybe do an NTB extension instead of or try to get it into NTB version 5 instead of a new protocol but some I believe something like rough is needed so if you want to talk about this well uh yes uh there was a new version submitted and it has improved the introduction hopefully better explaining the key differences between ntp and PTP and the big change was added support for PTP one step and to end transparent clocks this allows ntp clients to reach the same accuracy as PTP um the transparent clocks uh modify the correction field in the PTP delay request messages containing ntp messages and ntp clients can use this correction to correct the measured of sudden delay basically eliminating the impact of the network switches um and a new ntp extension field is required to return the correction of the request so the client has uh both the correction of the request and response and can check the their value before applying to the measured ofset And Delay um limit the impact of
